description_long: This is a pair of adjacent brick apartment buildings, each with a flat, weathered roof and a classic urban facade. The front faces feature large, arched and rectangular windows arranged in symmetrical rows, with the ground floor lined by commercial storefronts and awnings. The brickwork is a reddish-brown color, with subtle weathering and some visible details such as window sills and lintels. The sides and rear of the buildings are plain, with unadorned brick walls and no windows or decorative elements. The rooftops show signs of utility installations, such as vents and patches, typical of older city buildings. The overall appearance suggests early 20th-century industrial or warehouse conversion architecture, commonly found in dense urban neighborhoods.
description: A pair of classic brick apartment buildings with flat roofs, large front windows, and ground-floor storefronts, featuring symmetrical facades and weathered exteriors.
description_view_0: Front view showing two adjacent buildings with large arched and rectangular windows, ground-floor storefronts, and detailed brickwork.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain, windowless brick walls and the flat rooftops of both buildings.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the unadorned brick backs of the buildings, with no windows or entrances visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other plain, windowless brick wall and the flat rooftops.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical and horizontal rows, with arched windows on the upper floor and rectangular windows below, evenly spaced across the facade.
